channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google teams up with Samsung to take on Dolby Atmos
item.description: A helicopter flying over your head, the ground rumbling under your feet, bullets whizzing by your ears: If it’s up to Google and Samsung, then your movie nights will soon sound like you’re in the middle of the action.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Globe Life data breach may have affected 850,000 more patients than previously thought
item.description: Healthcare company initially said cyberattack affected 5,000 people, but stressed the number is likely bigger.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia and AMD are set for a showdown in March, if this rumor holds any truth - RTX 5060 Ti and 5060 reportedly launching next month
item.description: We're still waiting for a full reveal of AMD's Radeon RX 9000 series GPUs, but Nvidia could be poised to steal its thunder in March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: With his 70th birthday looming on this year’s horizon, Microsoft founder Bill Gates looked inward to open a window into the man behind Windows and other seminal software that turned the personal computer into a household staple.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried’s parents appealing to Trump to pardon their beloved son: report
item.description: The mother of Sam Bankman-Fried has apparently had a major change of heart about Trump after once leading a top Silicon Valley fundraising network for anti-Trump Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas. "We'll be doing pharmaceuticals. Importantly in drugs and medicines, etc. All forms of medicine and pharmaceuticals," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Are you problematic? This ‘cancel culture insurance’ could help you deal with social media backlash
item.description: Worried about getting canceled? There’s now insurance policy for that. Insurance agency Samphire Risk is rolling out “cancel culture” insurance for celebrities and execs afraid of social media backlash, according to a report from the Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Snowden speaks out on Gabbard confirmation battle
item.description: Former National Security Agency contractor Edward Snowden defended former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President Trump's nominee to be director of national intelligence, following a fiery confirmation hearing in the Senate on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: US Justice Department sues to block HPE’s $14 billion Juniper buy
item.description: *Article has been updated to include a joint response from HPE and Juniper to the U.S. DOJ complaint* After months of speculation, the U.S. Justice Department today sued to block the $14 billion sale of Juniper Networks to Hewlett Packard Enterprise.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA unveils AI Essentials training resource
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing how work gets done, creating new technology roles, and transforming the entire employment landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How DeepSeek will upend the AI industry
item.description: Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s cost-saving techniques for training and delivering generative AI (genAI) models could democratize the entire industry by lowering entry barriers for new AI companies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials probe China’s DeepSeek AI amid security and privacy scrutiny
item.description: The Chinese AI app DeepSeek has come under intense scrutiny from both the US and European regulators, raising alarms over national security risks, data privacy concerns, and potential intellectual property theft.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system
item.description: The plaintiffs claim OPM violated the E-Government Act by not releasing details of how the email system will manage federal employees’ personal information. The post Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
